title: JavaScript Conditionals Knowledge Check
description: Check your understanding of ternary expressions, truthy and falsy values, short-circuiting, nullish coalescing, and optional chaining.
author: DailyCoder

questions.prompt: What is the ternary operator best suited for?

options.text: Replacing every if...else statement in a program
Choosing between two simple expressions based on a condition
Running a loop until a condition becomes false

questions.explanation: A ternary is best for simple two-way choices, especially when assigning or returning a value.

questions.prompt:
What does this expression return?
```js
const result = 10 > 5 ? 'yes' : 'no';
```
questions.explanation: The condition `10 > 5` is true, so the expression after `?` is returned.

questions.prompt: Which value is falsy in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: `0` is falsy. Empty arrays, empty objects, and non-empty strings are truthy.

questions.prompt: Which list contains only falsy values?
questions.explanation: The common falsy values include `false`, `0`, `-0`, `0n`, empty string, `null`, `undefined`, and `NaN`.

questions.prompt:
What does this code log?

```js
console.log(Boolean('0'));
```
questions.explanation: The string `'0'` is non-empty, so it is truthy.

questions.prompt: What does the `&&` operator return when the first operand is falsy?
questions.explanation: `&&` short-circuits on the first falsy value and returns that actual value.
Because `'hello'` is truthy, `&&` evaluates and returns the second operand, `42`.

options.text: The first truthy operand, or the last operand if none are truthy

questions.explanation: `||` returns the first truthy value it finds. If every value is falsy, it returns the last value.

questions.prompt: Why can `||` be risky for default values?

options.text: It replaces valid falsy values like `0`, `''`, and `false`
It throws an error when the left side is `null`

questions.explanation: `||` uses truthiness, so valid values such as `0`, an empty string, or `false` can accidentally be replaced.
`0` is falsy, so `||` returns the fallback value `10`.

questions.prompt: When does the nullish coalescing operator (`??`) use the right-hand value?

options.text: When the left-hand value is any falsy value
Only when the left-hand value is `null` or `undefined`
Only when the left-hand value is an empty string
Only when the left-hand value is `false`

questions.explanation: `??` falls back only for `null` and `undefined`, preserving values like `0`, `''`, and `false`.
`0` is not nullish, so `??` keeps it.

questions.prompt: What problem does optional chaining (`?.`) help solve?

options.text: It safely reads nested properties when an earlier value may be `null` or `undefined`
It converts strings to numbers before comparison
It prevents all runtime errors in a program

questions.explanation: Optional chaining stops property access and returns `undefined` if the value before `?.` is `null` or `undefined`.

questions.prompt:
What does this code return if `user.profile` is `undefined`?

```js
user.profile?.email
```
questions.explanation: Because `profile` is `undefined`, optional chaining stops and returns `undefined` instead of trying to read `.email`.

questions.prompt: Which expression safely reads a user's city and falls back to `'Unknown'` only when the city is missing?
questions.explanation: `user?.address?.city` safely handles missing nested objects, and `??` only falls back when the city is `null` or `undefined`.
